lines: + * Tests that read preference metrics correctly count user-initiated commands routed through mongos
+ * as "external" rather than "internal" on the shard's mongod.
+ * Without the isExternalClientOnRouter flag, commands arriving from mongos are counted as "internal"
+ * because the mongos-to-mongod connection is an internal client. This test verifies that user
+ * commands through mongos correctly increment the "external" counter on the shard.
+// Insert documents so reads have something to find.
+// Shard the foreign collection across both shards so that a $lookup from mongod will need to
+// dispatch sub-operations to the other shard (mongod -> mongod path).
+// split at {_id: 0}, move the {_id: 1} chunk to the other shard.
+ * Verifies that a user command routed through mongos increments the "external" counter on the
+ * shard. We only assert that external increments by the expected amount; we do not assert that
+ * the internal counter is unchanged because unrelated background operations may legitimately
+ * increment it between our pre/post serverStatus reads.
+                `Expected external counter for ${readPref} on ${executedOn} to increment ` +
+jsTestLog("Testing that user commands through mongos count as external on shard primary");
+jsTestLog("Testing that sharded $lookup sub-operations propagate the external flag");
+    const pipeline = [{$lookup: {from: foreignCollName, localField: "x", foreignField: "x", as: "matched"}}];
+jsTestLog("Testing that $lookup sub-operations during getMore propagate the external flag");
+    const pipeline = [{$lookup: {from: foreignCollName, localField: "x", foreignField: "x", as: "matched"}}];
// This assert is here to make sure new command options are considered in the 'isReadOnly' check. If
// this assert fails, please make sure you consider the authorization implications of your change.
+            When enabled, mongos annotates commands forwarded on behalf of external
+            (user) clients with the isExternalClientOnRouter generic argument. This
+            allows mongod to correctly attribute those operations as external in read
+            preference metrics. Gated on FCV to ensure all nodes in the cluster
+            understand the field before it is sent.
// Skip incrementing metrics when the command is not a read operation, as we expect to all
// commands sent via the driver to inherit the read preference, even if we don't use it.
+            // Commands forwarded from a router (mongos) on behalf of an external user should
+            // be counted as "external", even though the mongos connection is an internal client.

+ * Returns a settable boolean indicating whether the given operation context originated from an
+ * external (user) client connection on a router (mongos).
+ * This flag is propagated from mongos to mongod so that the shard can distinguish user-initiated
+ * commands routed through mongos from internal system commands (e.g. resharding, balancer). Without
+ * this flag, all commands arriving from mongos are treated as "internal" because the mongos-to-
+ * mongod connection is an internal client, which causes read preference metrics to miscount
# it when forwarding the request to a shard or config server. See also: rawData.
+            # Set by the router when the command originates from an external (user) client
+            # connection. This allows mongod to distinguish user operations routed through
+            # mongos from internal system operations (e.g. resharding, balancer) for metrics
+            # purposes such as read preference counters.
+                # Manually appended by the egress metadata hook, not present in the original
+                # command BSON. See also: rawData, isDirectSystemBucketsAccess.
# Contains context fields of the current transaction
// If the request is using the 'defaultMaxTimeMS' value, attaches the field so shards can
